title: DPC Confirms Preliminary Finding Against Vantara AI
body: Ireland's Data Protection Commission confirmed Tuesday that it has issued a preliminary finding that Vantara AI breached GDPR rules governing the transfer of personal data to third countries. The DPC said Vantara now has four weeks to respond before a final decision is issued. The commission did not disclose the potential fine amount, but under GDPR, penalties can reach four percent of a company's global annual turnover. The preliminary finding validates the reporting EuroPrivacy Wire published in late February, which Vantara's CEO Nils Bergström called "inaccurate" at the time. Bergström had no immediate comment Tuesday. DataWatch EU called the finding "an important step" but noted that a preliminary decision is not a final order. The announcement came two days after Vantara said it would relocate European data infrastructure to Frankfurt and Amsterdam — a commitment the DPC acknowledged but said does not resolve the historical violation under review. Hanseong Group, which has a licensing agreement with Vantara, said it is monitoring the situation and that its own legal counsel has reviewed the arrangement.
